name: Ireland
avoid: July-August weekends sell out fast in Galway, Killarney, and Doolin — book rooms early.
Atlantic wind and rain can disrupt west-coast day trips even in summer.
stopoverNote: Fly into Dublin for the easiest car-free route; Shannon is handy only if you want to start on the west coast.

cities.name: Dublin
cities.notes: Trinity College, Guinness Storehouse, Georgian squares, pub culture and easy airport access

cities.name: Galway
cities.notes: Latin Quarter lanes, trad music pubs, Salthill promenade and west-coast base

cities.name: Cliffs of Moher
cities.notes: Iconic County Clare sea cliffs best paired with Doolin village and coastal walks

cities.name: Killarney
cities.notes: Killarney National Park, Muckross House, lakes and Ring of Kerry departures

cities.name: Cork
cities.notes: English Market food culture, compact centre and easy access to Blarney Castle

cities.name: Belfast
cities.notes: Titanic Quarter, Belfast City Hall and a straightforward rail day trip from Dublin

itinerary.sim: Three Ireland or Vodafone prepay SIM from Dublin Airport or city shops costs about ₹1,800 for a 28-day data-heavy plan that is enough for maps, ticket apps, and hotspot backup.

Dublin.name: Dublin
Dublin.note: Use Dublin to settle in, cover the big-ticket sights, and keep one flexible day for neighborhoods, museums, or an early Belfast run.

activities.name: Book of Kells Experience and Old Library at Trinity College
activities.tip: Book a morning timed slot because the Long Room gets crowded by late morning.

activities.name: Grafton Street buskers, Powerscourt Centre and St Stephen's Green walk
National Gallery of Ireland
Dublin Bus or Luas hops around the centre using a Leap card

hotels.name: Holiday Inn Express Dublin City Centre

activities.name: Guinness Storehouse self-guided visit with Gravity Bar pint
Kilmainham Gaol guided tour
activities.tip: Release-day tickets go fast, so reserve the exact date the moment sales open.

activities.name: St Patrick's Cathedral
Temple Bar and Ha'penny Bridge evening walk

hotels.name: Holiday Inn Express Dublin City Centre

activities.name: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum
Jeanie Johnston tall ship tour
DART or Dublin Bus outing to Howth for the harbour and cliff-side walk
Merrion Square and Georgian doors photo walk

hotels.name: Holiday Inn Express Dublin City Centre

Galway.name: Galway
Galway.note: Galway is compact enough for a short stay but deserves two days if you want both city atmosphere and one worthwhile excursion.

activities.name: Latin Quarter, Quay Street and Spanish Arch wander
Galway City Museum
Salthill Promenade walk to the Blackrock diving tower
Evening trad session hopping around Tig Cóilí or The Crane Bar

days.theme: Aran Islands Option or Slow Galway Day

activities.name: Aran Island Ferries return sailing to Inis Mór from Rossaveal
activities.tip: Go only with a good forecast because Atlantic swell can make crossings rough.

activities.name: Bike or minibus around Inis Mór to Dún Aonghasa
Alternative no-ferry day: Galway Cathedral, university quarter and weekend market browsing

Cliffs of Moher.name: Cliffs of Moher
Cliffs of Moher.note: A one-night stop in Doolin gives you a calmer Cliffs visit than a rushed day trip from Dublin and keeps you well placed for onward travel south.

activities.name: Bus Éireann Route 350 between Galway, Doolin and the cliffs
Cliffs of Moher Experience visitor centre entry
O'Brien's Tower and the marked cliff walk toward Doolin
Doolin village evening trad music session

hotels.name: Aille River Tourist Hostel & Camping Doolin

Killarney.name: Killarney
Killarney.note: Killarney works best with two nights so you can split national-park sights from the full Ring of Kerry circuit.

activities.name: Muckross House and Gardens
Muckross Abbey
Ross Castle guided visit
Cycle or walk sections of Killarney National Park
Coach-based Ring of Kerry day tour from Killarney
activities.tip: A guided coach avoids narrow-road stress and usually includes photo stops at Ladies View, Sneem and Waterville.

activities.name: Torc Waterfall stop if self-driving or on selected tours
Evening lakefront stroll back in town

Cork.name: Cork
Cork.note: One full day covers central Cork plus Blarney Castle; stay longer only if you want a separate Cobh or Kinsale outing.

activities.name: English Market breakfast and produce walk
Blarney Castle and Gardens entry
Cork City Gaol
Shandon Bells and St Anne's Church tower climb

hotels.name: Maldron Hotel South Mall Cork City

meals: Elbow Lane Brew and Smoke House

Belfast.name: Belfast
Belfast.note: Treat Belfast as a long but manageable day trip from Dublin by Enterprise train, or stay one night if you want a slower Titanic Quarter morning.

activities.name: Enterprise train day trip between Dublin Connolly and Belfast Lanyon Place
Titanic Belfast self-guided experience
Belfast City Hall exterior and Cathedral Quarter walk
Black Taxi mural tour

hotels.name: Ramada by Wyndham Belfast City Centre

connections.method: Irish Rail direct from Dublin Heuston to Galway Ceannt or GoBus coach from the city/airport, about 2.5-3 hours
Bus Éireann Route 350 via Lisdoonvarna and Doolin, about 2-2.5 hours
Bus Éireann to Ennis or Limerick, then onward rail or coach to Killarney, about 4.5-5.5 hours
Irish Rail direct to Cork Kent or Bus Éireann coach, about 1.5-2 hours
Irish Rail to Dublin, transfer across the city, then Enterprise train to Belfast, about 5.5-6.5 hours total

extras: A 72-hour Leap Visitor Card for Dublin Bus, Luas and DART is about ₹1,440 and is the easiest way to avoid paying single fares in central Dublin.
Belfast prices are locally charged in GBP, but the itinerary above converts them to INR for consistency.
If you do not want to change hotels for Belfast, keep your Dublin base and take the first Enterprise train out and an evening return.
Hotel Doolin, Galway, and Killarney often jump sharply on summer weekends and bank holidays, so lock those stays in first.
A car is useful for the southwest but not essential; this route is written to stay workable with Irish Rail, GoBus, Dublin Bus and Bus Éireann.

Dublin: Trinity College Dublin Long Room Library
Cliffs of Moher: Cliffs of Moher Atlantic sea stack
